---
title: R for the humanities
description: An introductory [![](img/logo_r.png){width="1.4em"}](https://www.r-project.org/) course for the humanities and social sciences
title-block-banner: true
---

:::{.topdef}

R is a free and open-source programming language for statistical computing, modelling, and graphics, with an unbeatable collection of statistical packages. It is extremely popular in the humanities and social sciences.

This course does not assume any programming knowledge.

:::

<br>
[[Start course ➤](hss_why.qmd)]{.topinline}

<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "LearningResource",
  "name": "R for the humanities",
  "description": "An introduction to R: IDEs, syntax, data types, indexing, function definition, control flow, packages, data exploration, and plotting.",
  "url": "https://mint.westdri.ca/r/top_hss",
  "learningResourceType": "course",
  "inLanguage": "en",
  "license": "https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/",
  "provider": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "SFU Research Computing Group",
  },
  "keywords": "R, intro, humanities, HSS",
  "author": {
    "@type": "Person",
    "name": "Marie-Hélène Burle"
  }
}
</script>
